title: rubus glandulosus subsp. incanescens
intro: Rubus glandulosus subsp. incanescens, also known as the bramble, is a subspecies of a flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Rubus glandulosus subsp. incanescens include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal infusions and topical applications.
